Cases 10 12.1 General Conditions 10 13 Questions and Answers 10 Enhancement Revision History Changed By Date Version Notes: Brian Brazon 5/24/2022 0.0 Original document. EX55 – Cycle Count Recount Acceptance Enchantement McLane ’s requires the recount process for cycle counts to be accepted upon a met variance from previous count or current quantity at the location. EX55 introd uces logic to accept count values upon met configured variance if count falls outside of base variance for initial cycle count and recount tasks . The recount process is enhanced to eliminate the number of recounts required before the cycle count is accepted and the location is updated. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other Y Assumptions No changes are made to the cycle count screen. No customization to Labor Management is done as part of this extension. No customization to Activity Tracking is done as part of this extension. No customization to PIX generation is done as part of this extension. EX55 must be able to handle Mix-SKU, Multi-deep locations, Expiry tracked SKUs, reserve, active and case pick locations. No changes are made to cycle count reports. Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram Process Steps Process Steps: System generated cycle count task is created. Cycle counts are created and completed through standard Base flows Base validatio n ensure s that counts are booked when no variance exists on the location Base validation checks that the count is within tolerance for variances , booking immediately or creating a recount task if out of tolerance which is then completed by a user in a different task group Base validation ensure s that counts are booked when no variance exists on the location for the recount task, regardless of what variance existed on the first co unt Base validation checks that the count is within tolerance for variances, booking immediately if within tolerance of the location in ventory regardless of what variance existed on the first count An additional Custom validation is added to book out of tolerance against inventory recounts, if the recount variance is within a custom secondary tolerance of the first count variance . If the second count is within tolerance of the first count, then the count is booked Else WM generates a 2 nd count task as custom, to be the 3 rd and final count on the loc ation in the process The final count is performed through the same Base flow and task group as the initial recount task , and Base validation ensures that counts are booked when no variance exists on the location for the final task, regardless of what variance existed on the first or second count Base validation checks that the count is within tolerance for variances, booking immediately if within tolerance of the location inventory regardless of what variance existed on the first or second count Two additional Custom validations are added to book out of tolerance against inventory recounts, if the recount variance is within a custom secondary tolerance of the first or second count variance. If the final count is within tolerance of the first count, then the count is booked Else if the final count is within tolerance of the second count (initial recount) , then the count is booked Else WM stops the chained recount generation process and records this final count in Deferred status The final count recorded in Deferred status is t o be researched by the inventory control team and booked or rejected through the Cycle Counts UI Us er enters System Directed Cycle Count transaction. User prompted to scan location and item barcode. User inputs current quantity at th e location. If, tolerance for recount is exceeded, recount task is generated, EX55 custom flow is invoked. Else, no task is generated count is met and cycle count is booked. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No changes to base behavior for PIX generation as part of this extension. RF No changes to RF behavior as part of this extension. Process Change/Addition Enablement IF C-00 0 -000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement) IF C-00 0 - EX 55 byte 1 = Y ( extension specific enablement) IF Cycle Count transaction parameter 50 2 -50 2 = ‘Y’ THEN the functionality detailed in this section is enabled ELSE the functionality detailed in the section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in section 7 .1 .2 is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in section 7 .1 .2 is disabled Cycle Count Recount Acceptance McLane utilizes base cycle count creation to generate cycle counts for specific locations. EX55 is used to customize the recount process for the McLane business team after a recount is generated upon a variance threshold being met. EX55 is customized to handle multiple scenarios upon recounts being generated and cycle counts being booked . Those scenarios are explained in detail below , all custom logic is bold : Cycle Count Scenario 1: First Cycle Count task IF cycle count is equal to system WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY THEN: WM accepts the count and makes no changes to the WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY at the location. ELSE IF the cycle count is or within tolerance set (x ‌ < tolerance) of the WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY , THEN: WM accept s the count and make s no immediate changes to the WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY at the location. ELSE THEN: WM creates a recount task and , continue s to second cycle scenario Example 1 : Example 1 : Above example demonstrates that if counted value is equal to ON_HAND_QTY or within tolerance , then no recount tasks are generated, and counts are booked. Else, the variance falls outside of the tolerance and recount tasks are generated. (Green demonstrates within tolerance booked values) Scenario 1: Count equal to On Hand Qty Scenario 2 : Count within tolerance of On Hand Qty Cycle Count Scenario 2: First Cycle Count Task was is generated; count is not equal to WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY and outside of variance. R ecount task is generated , upon completion recount task is updated with first count s PHYS_INVN_HDR . PHYS _INVN_HDR_ID task ID in TASK_HDR.TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR once complete , linking second re count task to first count task . IF Cycle Count is not equal to or within the variance of ` tolerance set (x > tolerance). T HEN : WM generates a recount task and recount task process is followed. IF r ecount task is not equal to or within tolerance set (x ‌ < tolerance) of initial cycle count THEN: second recount task is generated and continue to scenario 3. ELSE IF recount task is equal to or within tolerance set (x > tolerance) of initial cycle count or WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY . THEN: WM accepts count and recount task is booked. ELSE , continue to third cycle count scenario. Example 2 : Above example demonstrates that if counted value for a cycle count is not within or equal to tolerance, then a recount task is generated . U ser will count s location again . Scenario 3 & 4 : If counts are equal to or within the tolerance of location on hand qty Cycle Count 1 then counts are booked. Green values demonstrate the match values between cycle count tasks. Scenario 5: If counts are equal to or within the tolerance of first cycle count th en cycle count is booked. (Green demonstrates within tolerance booked values) Example 3 : Above example demonstrates that if counted value is not within or equal to tolerance of first cycle count, then a recount task is generated. Upon user completing recount, tolerance is not within or equal to Cycle Count 1 task. Second recount task is generated and scenario 3 is invoked , continue below as per custom flow . Cycle Count Scenario 3: Second recount task is generated due to first two scenarios not being met. R ecount task is updated with first count PHYS_INVN_HDR .PHYS_INVN_HDR_ID task ID in TASK_HDR.TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR once complete linking 3 rd count task to first task . WM checks to see if second r ec ount task counted value is equal to or within the tolerance set (x > tolerance) configured in C-E55- TOL original cycle count and first recount task . IF counted value for task is equal to or within tolerance set (x ‌ < tolerance) of first cycle count task THEN ; WM books count , and recount task is complete . ELSE IF counted value for task is equal to or within tolerance set (x ‌ < tolerance) of first recount task THEN ; WM books count , and recount task is complete . ELSE IF count is not equal to or within tolerance set (x ‌ < tolerance) of original cycle count, and first recount task THEN ; second recount task is updated to complete status and count status goes to differed deferred , supervisor then books the count upon confirmation of count . Else, WM skips this logic due to no additional recount tasks being generated. Example 3 : Above example demonstrates that if counted value for a cycle count is not within or equal to tolerance, then a recount task is generated. User count’s location again for a third time. Scenario 6 & 7 : If counts are equal to or within the tolerance of location on hand qty then counts are booked. If counts are equal to or within the tolerance of first cycle count, then cycle count is booked. Scenario 8 & 9: If counts are equal to or within the tolerance of second cycle count, then cycle count is booked. (Green demonstrates within tolerance booked values) Example 4 : Upon generation of second recount task , user counts units and EX55 invokes custom logic to compare with tolerance configured in C-E55-TOL . EX55 compares counted value to both cycle count task 1 and recount task 1. Unit Variance is equal to that of recount task 1 therefore count is booked. Example 5 : Upon generation of second recount task, user counts units and EX55 invokes custom logic to compare with tolerance configured in C-E55-TOL . EX55 compares counted value to both cycle count task 1 and recount task 1. Unit Variance is equal to that of cycle count task 1 therefore count is booked. Examp l e 4 6 : Variance is not equal to or within On hand qty, cycle count task 1 or recount task 1 . Cycle Count task is complete and cycle count is differed. therefore cycle count is in booked status and task is sent to complete status. Supervisor must come and book with final count. Note: excel file with charts attached below. Validations The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 55 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 55 is turned off, and no custom EX 55 processing will occur. Thirdly , the extension specific transaction parameter flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom tran-parm 502-502 . This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 55 is turned off, and no custom EX 55 processing will occur. Lastly , WM determines initial cycle count task using TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR from the completed cycle count task. TASK_HDR.TASK_DESC = SYS_CODE.CODE_DESC matching the task type with SYS_CODE.CODE_ID from sys code S 590 in (’ 03 , ‘ 09 ’, ‘ 10 ’, ‘ 54 ’, ’ 93 ) . Upon competition of first cycle count task, WM looks up corresponding cycle count tasks for location less than or equal to 30 days. SELECT DATE_SUB(" SYSDATE ", INTERVAL 30 DAY); IF user performs user directed cycle count or WM finds cycle count tasks completed with TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR = ‘NULL’ . upon completion WM finds no corresponding cycle coun t tasks for the last 30 days and TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR = ‘NULL’ upon task completion. THEN : WM determines this cycle count task to be cycle count task 1 and EX55 custom logic is invoked. ELSE IF a recount task is generated and completed w here TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR is not ‘NULL’ . THEN: WM determines this as the second cycle count task where total number of tasks with TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR is not ‘NULL’ = 1 for the location . upon completion a recount task is generated TASK_HDR.TASK_DESC = SYS_CODE.CODE_DESC matching the task type with SYS_CODE.CODE_ID from sys code S 590 in ( ’27, ‘28’, ‘30’, ‘31’, ’55) and cycle count task found from previous 30 days with corresponding location with variance . THEN: WM determines this cycle count task to be recount task 1 and EX55 custom logic is invoked, and updates are made as called out in section 7.1.4. ELSE IF a second recount task is generated and completed and TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_RE F _NBR is not ‘NULL’. ELSE IF upon completion a recount task is generated TASK_HDR.TASK_DESC = SYS_CODE.CODE_DESC matching the task type with SYS_CODE.CODE_ID from sys code S 590 in (’27, ‘28’, ‘30’, ‘31’, ’55) and cycle count task found from previous 30 days with corresponding location with variance . THEN: WM determines this to be the third cycle count task to be recount task and EX55 custom logic is invoked, and updates are made as called out in section 7.1.4 . where total number of tasks with TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR is not ‘NULL’ > 1 for the location . ELSE, recount task is not generated, and cycle count is booked booked, and task is complete. Updates WM finds associated task with location and updates PHYS_INVN_HDR .PHYS_INVN_HDR_ID TASK_ID to TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR on current recount task being complete . Outputs Activity Tracking There are no changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX There are no changes to base PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks There are no changes to base labor hooks as part of this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. Menu Parameters Parameter (Category > Parameter) Value Warehouse Master Parameter (Tab > Section > Parameter) Value System Codes Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description Frm To Description Validation Type Configured value C 000 000 Global Extension Control Global Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – Global Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX 55 EX 55 Extension Control EX 55 Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX 55 Extension Control is turned ON C E55 TOL EX55 Extension Control EX55 Tolerance Value 1 2 1 Enablement Flag Tolerance Percentage Edit Box 0-99 N/A Database Not required for this extension. Custom Configurations System Code Types Rec Type Code Type Code Desc RF Desc Max Len. EX55 is used to customize the recount process for the McLane business team after a recount is generated upon a variance threshold being met. EX55 is customized to handle multiple scenarios upon recounts being generated and cycle counts being booked . Those scenarios are explained in detail below , all custom logic is bold : Cycle Count Scenario 1: First Cycle Count task IF cycle count is equal to system WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY THEN: WM accepts the count and makes no changes to the WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY at the location. ELSE IF the cycle count is or within tolerance set (x ‌ < tolerance) of the WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY , THEN: WM accept s the count and make s no immediate changes to the WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY at the location. ELSE THEN: WM creates a recount task and , continue s to second cycle scenario Example 1 : Example 1 : Above example demonstrates that if counted value is equal to ON_HAND_QTY or within tolerance , then no recount tasks are generated, and counts are booked. Else, the variance falls outside of the tolerance and recount tasks are generated. (Green demonstrates within tolerance booked values) Scenario 1: Count equal to On Hand Qty Scenario 2 : Count within tolerance of On Hand Qty Cycle Count Scenario 2: First Cycle Count Task was is generated; count is not equal to WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY and outside of variance. R ecount task is generated , upon completion recount task is updated with first count s PHYS_INVN_HDR . PHYS _INVN_HDR_ID task ID in TASK_HDR.TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR once complete , linking second re count task to first count task . IF Cycle Count is not equal to or within the variance of ` tolerance set (x > tolerance).

IF r ecount task is not equal to or within tolerance set (x ‌ < tolerance) of initial cycle count THEN: second recount task is generated and continue to scenario 3. ELSE IF recount task is equal to or within tolerance set (x > tolerance) of initial cycle count or WM_INVENTORY.ON_HAND_QTY . THEN: WM accepts count and recount task is booked. ELSE , continue to third cycle count scenario. Example 2 : Above example demonstrates that if counted value for a cycle count is not within or equal to tolerance, then a recount task is generated . ELSE IF counted value for task is equal to or within tolerance set (x ‌ < tolerance) of first recount task THEN ; WM books count , and recount task is complete . Example 3 : Above example demonstrates that if counted value for a cycle count is not within or equal to tolerance, then a recount task is generated. User count’s location again for a third time. Scenario 6 & 7 : If counts are equal to or within the tolerance of location on hand qty then counts are booked. If counts are equal to or within the tolerance of first cycle count, then cycle count is booked. Scenario 8 & 9: If counts are equal to or within the tolerance of second cycle count, then cycle count is booked. (Green demonstrates within tolerance booked values) Example 4 : Upon generation of second recount task , user counts units and EX55 invokes custom logic to compare with tolerance configured in C-E55-TOL . EX55 compares counted value to both cycle count task 1 and recount task 1. Unit Variance is equal to that of recount task 1 therefore count is booked. Example 5 : Upon generation of second recount task, user counts units and EX55 invokes custom logic to compare with tolerance configured in C-E55-TOL . EX55 compares counted value to both cycle count task 1 and recount task 1. Unit Variance is equal to that of cycle count task 1 therefore count is booked. Examp l e 4 6 : Variance is not equal to or within On hand qty, cycle count task 1 or recount task 1 . Cycle Count task is complete and cycle count is differed. therefore cycle count is in booked status and task is sent to complete status. Supervisor must come and book with final count. Note: excel file with charts attached below. Validations The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 55 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 55 is turned off, and no custom EX 55 processing will occur. Thirdly , the extension specific transaction parameter flag must be enabled.

[Document: MCLN EX55 – Cycle Count Recount Acceptance Enhancement]

flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 55 is turned off, and no custom EX 55 processing will occur. Thirdly , the extension specific transaction parameter flag must be enabled.

This is controlled through the custom tran-parm 502-502 . This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 55 is turned off, and no custom EX 55 processing will occur. Lastly , WM determines initial cycle count task using TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR from the completed cycle count task. TASK_HDR.TASK_DESC = SYS_CODE.CODE_DESC matching the task type with SYS_CODE.CODE_ID from sys code S 590 in (’ 03 , ‘ 09 ’, ‘ 10 ’, ‘ 54 ’, ’ 93 ) . Upon competition of first cycle count task, WM looks up corresponding cycle count tasks for location less than or equal to 30 days. SELECT DATE_SUB(" SYSDATE ", INTERVAL 30 DAY); IF user performs user directed cycle count or WM finds cycle count tasks completed with TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR = ‘NULL’ . upon completion WM finds no corresponding cycle coun t tasks for the last 30 days and TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR = ‘NULL’ upon task completion. THEN : WM determines this cycle count task to be cycle count task 1 and EX55 custom logic is invoked. ELSE IF a recount task is generated and completed w here TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR is not ‘NULL’ . THEN: WM determines this as the second cycle count task where total number of tasks with TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR is not ‘NULL’ = 1 for the location . upon completion a recount task is generated TASK_HDR.TASK_DESC = SYS_CODE.CODE_DESC matching the task type with SYS_CODE.CODE_ID from sys code S 590 in ( ’27, ‘28’, ‘30’, ‘31’, ’55) and cycle count task found from previous 30 days with corresponding location with variance . THEN: WM determines this cycle count task to be recount task 1 and EX55 custom logic is invoked, and updates are made as called out in section 7.1.4. ELSE IF a second recount task is generated and completed and TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_RE F _NBR is not ‘NULL’. ELSE IF upon completion a recount task is generated TASK_HDR.TASK_DESC = SYS_CODE.CODE_DESC matching the task type with SYS_CODE.CODE_ID from sys code S 590 in (’27, ‘28’, ‘30’, ‘31’, ’55) and cycle count task found from previous 30 days with corresponding location with variance . THEN: WM determines this to be the third cycle count task to be recount task and EX55 custom logic is invoked, and updates are made as called out in section 7.1.4 . where total number of tasks with TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR is not ‘NULL’ > 1 for the location .

[Document: MCLN EX55 – Cycle Count Recount Acceptance Enhancement]

responding location with variance . THEN: WM determines this to be the third cycle count task to be recount task and EX55 custom logic is invoked, and updates are made as called out in section 7.1.4 . where total number of tasks with TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR is not ‘NULL’ > 1 for the location .

ELSE, recount task is not generated, and cycle count is booked booked, and task is complete. Updates WM finds associated task with location and updates PHYS_INVN_HDR .PHYS_INVN_HDR_ID TASK_ID to TASK_HDR. TASK_CMPL_REF_NBR on current recount task being complete . Outputs Activity Tracking There are no changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX There are no changes to base PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks There are no changes to base labor hooks as part of this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension.
